exactBlocker: BUILDSTATE marked M6 Campaigns as blocked because actual bulk/list email execution is gated by SPF/DKIM/DMARC confirmation, tenant physical mailing address, and explicit owner approval. That is an external send boundary, not a missing campaign-build capability.
resolution: M6 module status is separated from outbound execution. Campaign generation, audience query, XState sequencing, compliance injection, suppression, tracking, templates, transactional queueing, AccessContext gates, and ApprovalQueue-only behavior are treated as the build scope. Actual bulk/list execution remains parked until the external send boundary is cleared.

dnsRecon.reasonExecutionStillBlocked: Public DNS presence is not enough to enable sends. M6 requires intentional staging env confirmation and physical mailing address configuration before any executor may consider list execution.

fixPathBeforeBulkExecution: Set M6_SPF_CONFIRMED=true only after owner confirms SPF record is correct for the sending domain.
Set M6_DKIM_CONFIRMED=true only after owner confirms Google Workspace DKIM is active for the sending domain.
Set M6_DMARC_CONFIRMED=true only after owner confirms DMARC policy/reporting is acceptable.
Set M6_PHYSICAL_ADDRESS or TENANT_PHYSICAL_ADDRESS to the legal mailing address required in list email footers.
Keep all sends ApprovalQueue-gated and require explicit owner approval before any executor sends.
part9RealityGate: M6 build-to-approval is done; bulk/list execution remains a parked outbound boundary, not a code blocker.
